title: Concentra to Announce First Quarter 2026 Results on Thursday, May 7, 2026
content:
ADDISON, Texas, April 09, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Concentra® Group Holdings Parent, Inc. ("Concentra") (NYSE: CON) will release the financial results for its first quarter ended March 31, 2026, on Thursday, May 7, 2026, after the market closes.

Concentra will host a conference call regarding its financial results on Friday, May 8, 2026, at 9 a.m. Eastern Time. The conference call will be a live webcast and can be accessed via this Earnings Call Webcast Link or via Concentra’s website at https://ir.concentra.com. A replay of the webcast will be available shortly after the call at the same locations.

Participants may join the audio-only version of the webcast or participate in the question-and-answer session by calling:

Toll Free: 888-506-0062
International: 973-528-0011
Participant Access Code: 873704

About Concentra

Concentra is the largest provider of occupational health services in the United States by number of locations, with the mission of improving the health of America’s workforce, one patient at a time. Our approximately 13,000 colleagues and affiliated physicians and clinicians support the delivery of an extensive suite of services, including occupational and consumer health services and other direct-to-employer care. We support the care of approximately 53,000 patients each business day on average across 47 states and the District of Columbia at our 628 occupational health centers, 411 onsite health clinics at employer worksites, and Concentra Telemed as of December 31, 2025.

www.Concentra.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260409436219/en/

Contacts

214-596-8735 
annakleiner@concentra.com

title: 3 European Stocks Estimated To Be Undervalued By Up To 48.7%
content:
As European markets experience a surge in optimism, with the STOXX Europe 600 Index gaining nearly 4% amid hopes of a swift resolution to Middle East tensions, investors are increasingly on the lookout for stocks that may be undervalued amidst this buoyant environment. Identifying such opportunities requires careful consideration of factors like market volatility and energy price impacts, which can reveal stocks trading below their intrinsic value despite broader economic challenges.

Top 10 Undervalued Stocks Based On Cash Flows In Europe

Name Current Price Fair Value (Est) Discount (Est) Vitrolife (OM:VITR) SEK95.25 SEK188.41 49.4% Talgo (BME:TLGO) €2.84 €5.66 49.8% Nordisk Bergteknik (OM:NORB B) SEK11.35 SEK22.55 49.7% Mare Group (BIT:MARE) €3.42 €6.80 49.7% Haypp Group (OM:HAYPP) SEK120.00 SEK236.32 49.2% Demant (CPSE:DEMANT) DKK191.80 DKK381.97 49.8% Casta Diva Group (BIT:CDG) €3.07 €6.13 50% B&S Group (ENXTAM:BSGR) €5.85 €11.66 49.8% Atea (OB:ATEA) NOK143.80 NOK286.40 49.8% Allegro.eu (WSE:ALE) PLN26.77 PLN52.83 49.3%

Click here to see the full list of 186 stocks from our Undervalued European Stocks Based On Cash Flows screener.

Let's dive into some prime choices out of the screener.

Digi Communications

Overview: Digi Communications N.V. offers a range of telecommunication services including cable TV, fixed internet and data, fixed-line and mobile telephony, and direct-to-home television in Romania and Spain, with mobile services also in Italy, and has a market cap of RON14.17 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue primarily from wireless communications services, amounting to €2.21 billion.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 26%

Digi Communications is trading at RON49.5, which is 26% below its estimated fair value of RON66.87 based on discounted cash flow analysis, indicating undervaluation. Despite a recent net loss of €33.02 million for 2025, earnings are forecast to grow significantly at 32.6% annually over the next three years, outpacing market averages. However, challenges include low profit margins and insufficient earnings coverage for interest payments following a recent stock split and dividend distribution on April 7, 2026.

Our growth report here indicates Digi Communications may be poised for an improving outlook. Get an in-depth perspective on Digi Communications' balance sheet by reading our health report here.BVB:DIGI Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

AutoStore Holdings

Overview: AutoStore Holdings Ltd. is a company that offers robotic and software technology solutions across various regions including Norway, Germany, Europe, the United States, and Asia with a market cap of NOK35.28 billion.

Story Continues

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily derived from its Industrial Automation & Controls segment, which generated $538.60 million.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 47.2%

AutoStore Holdings is trading at NOK10.49, significantly below its estimated fair value of NOK19.85, suggesting undervaluation based on discounted cash flow analysis. Despite a decline in net income to US$81.8 million for 2025, earnings are expected to grow substantially at 27.2% annually over the next three years, surpassing Norwegian market averages. However, challenges include reduced profit margins from 22.7% to 15.2% and a volatile share price in recent months.

According our earnings growth report, there's an indication that AutoStore Holdings might be ready to expand. Click to explore a detailed breakdown of our findings in AutoStore Holdings' balance sheet health report.OB:AUTO Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Continental

Overview: Continental Aktiengesellschaft manufactures tires and develops solutions for automotive manufacturers, industrial, and end customers worldwide, with a market cap of €13.17 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily derived from its Tires segment, which generated €13.80 billion, and its ContiTech segment, which contributed €6.01 billion.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 48.7%

Continental Aktiengesellschaft is trading at €65.86, significantly below its estimated future cash flow value of €128.44, highlighting potential undervaluation based on discounted cash flow analysis. Despite a net loss of €165 million in 2025 and forecasted revenue decline of 1.6% annually over the next three years, earnings are expected to grow by 57.14% per year as profitability improves, though dividends remain poorly covered by earnings or free cash flows.

Our earnings growth report unveils the potential for significant increases in Continental's future results. Unlock comprehensive insights into our analysis of Continental stock in this financial health report.XTRA:CON Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026
